api/spec/packages/aip/src/features.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the v3 Features domain: meter-backed feature models with optional unit costs, cost-query types, and CRUD+query operations compiled to OpenAPI and Go/JS/Python SDKs. All types live under namespace Features.

patterns.name: Namespace isolation
patterns.description: Every .tsp file opens with namespace Features; so generated names stay scoped.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for polymorphic types
patterns.description: Unions use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) so the OpenAPI discriminator is a top-level type field with no envelope wrapping (FeatureUnitCost: manual | llm).
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union FeatureUnitCost { manual: FeatureManualUnitCost, llm: FeatureLlmUnitCost }

patterns.name: Visibility annotations control lifecycle access
patterns.description: Fields use @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, ...) to control HTTP exposure. Computed read-only fields (resolved pricing) use @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only.

patterns.name: Extension markers for unstable/internal endpoints
patterns.description: Non-public operations carry extension markers. All current feature operations carry @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true).

patterns.name: Shared generic response wrappers
patterns.description: Operations return Shared.PagePaginatedResponse<T>, Shared.CreateResponse<T>, Shared.UpdateResponse<T>, Shared.GetResponse<T>, Shared.DeleteResponse — never inline response shapes.

patterns.name: Suppress linter rules with inline justification
patterns.description: When a name/shape legitimately violates @openmeter/api-spec-aip (nullable, repeated-prefix grouping, shared-model doc), suppress with #suppress and a quoted reason.

patterns.name: deepObject+explode for filter query params
patterns.description: List filter params use @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) with a dedicated *ParamsFilter model of Common.*FieldFilter fields.

key_file_guides.role: FeatureOperations (list/create/get/update(PATCH)/delete) and FeatureCostOperations (POST /query) interfaces with route params, filters, and response types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and use `using TypeSpec.Http;`. queryCost reuses Meters.MeterQueryRequest as the body.

key_file_guides.role: FeatureUnitCost discriminated union (manual/llm), FeatureUnitCostType / FeatureLlmTokenType enums, and FeatureLlmUnitCostPricing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mutually exclusive fields (provider vs provider_property, model vs model_property, token_type vs token_type_property) are docs-only — no TypeSpec constraint; add #suppress for repeated-prefix-grouping where flagged.

key_file_guides.role: Feature model (spreads Shared.ResourceWithKey, optional meter FeatureMeterReference + unit_cost) and FeatureUpdateRequest.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FeatureUpdateRequest includes only updatable fields (unit_cost, nullable to clear); do not add read-only fields like id or created_at.

key_file_guides.role: FeatureCostQueryRow and FeatureCostQueryResult; reuses Meters.MeterQueryRequest as request body.
key_file_guides.watch_for: cost is Shared.Numeric | null and requires the no-nullable #suppress; the detail field explains why cost is null.

key_file_guides.role: Barrel importing cost/unitcost/operations/feature and reopening namespace Features.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files must be added here.

anti_patterns: Declaring HTTP routes directly in model files — routes belong in operations.tsp only.
Defining response envelope shapes inline instead of using Shared.CreateResponse<T>/PagePaginatedResponse<T>.
Adding @visibility(Lifecycle.Create) to computed/server-set fields (id, created_at, status, resolved pricing).
Omitting the appropriate extension markers on internal-only operations.
Editing generated api/v3/api.gen.go instead of regenerating after TypeSpec changes.

decisions.decision: LLM unit cost is a flat model with mutually exclusive static/property fields, not a nested union.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the API surface flat and JSON-friendly; TypeSpec unions cannot enforce mutual exclusion, so the constraint is documented and validated in the Go service layer.
Cost can be unresolvable when LLM pricing data is unavailable; null is the correct semantic rather than omitting the field, even though it violates no-nullable.

code_examples.scenario: Adding an operation to FeatureOperations in operations.tsp

api/spec/packages/aip/src/governance.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the v3 Governance domain: a batch feature-access query that resolves customer identifiers to per-feature access states with partial-error semantics and cursor pagination. All operations are private, unstable, and internal.

patterns.name: Partial-error response envelope
patterns.description: GovernanceQueryResponse combines data (successful results), errors (per-identifier failures, typed via GovernanceQueryErrorCode), and meta (Common.CursorMeta) — enabling partial success without HTTP 207.
patterns.example: model GovernanceQueryResponse { data: GovernanceQueryResult[]; errors: GovernanceQueryError[]; meta: Common.CursorMeta; }

patterns.name: BaseError extension for typed error/reason bodies
patterns.description: GovernanceQueryError and GovernanceFeatureAccessReason both use model is Shared.BaseError<EnumCode>; GovernanceQueryError adds an optional customer field.

patterns.name: Record<T> for dynamic feature maps
patterns.description: GovernanceQueryResult.features is Record<GovernanceFeatureAccess> keyed by feature key, allowing a variable number of features per customer.

patterns.name: All operations private/unstable/internal
patterns.description: GovernanceOperations carries all three extension markers — the most restrictive visibility combination in the spec.

patterns.name: Cursor pagination on a POST body query
patterns.description: The query operation spreads ...Common.CursorPaginationQuery as query params alongside a @body GovernanceQueryRequest — paginating batch responses while keeping the (potentially large) request in the body.

patterns.name: Unknown sentinel on error enums
patterns.description: Both GovernanceFeatureAccessReasonCode and GovernanceQueryErrorCode include an Unknown: "unknown" zero-value sentinel for forward compatibility.

key_file_guides.role: All governance models: GovernanceQueryRequest (+Customers/Features), GovernanceQueryResponse/Result, GovernanceFeatureAccess, reason/error codes and BaseError types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: matched on GovernanceQueryResult is a string[] of all identifiers resolving to one customer — do not collapse to a single string. Customer keys are @minItems(1)@maxItems(100).

key_file_guides.role: GovernanceOperations interface with the single POST /query endpoint.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and use `using TypeSpec.Http;`. The POST needs both spread cursor params and a @body.

key_file_guides.role: Barrel importing governance.tsp and operations.tsp, reopening namespace Governance.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files must be added here.

anti_patterns: Returning HTTP 207 Multi-Status instead of the data+errors+meta partial-success envelope.
Using a GET with query params for the query — the request body (up to 100 customer keys) needs POST.
Omitting any of the three extension markers (Private/Unstable/Internal) on new governance operations.
Adding optional fields to GovernanceQueryRequest without default values (include_credits defaults to false is the precedent).

decisions.decision: Single POST /query with a partial-error response rather than per-customer GET endpoints.
decisions.rationale: Batch evaluation of up to 100 customers across all features in one round-trip is a latency requirement; partial errors (CustomerNotFound) must not abort the whole response.

decisions.decision: Unknown sentinel value on all error code enums.
decisions.rationale: Forward compatibility — new server-side codes degrade gracefully to Unknown on older clients rather than failing to deserialize.

api/spec/packages/aip/src/invoices.purpose: TypeSpec type definitions for invoice-related models (InvoiceNumber scalar, BillingParty, tax identity, addresses) shared across v3 billing operations. This folder contains only type definitions — invoice CRUD operations live in the billing/ namespace.

patterns.name: Scalar types for domain-constrained strings
patterns.description: Domain identifiers use scalar X extends string with @minLength/@maxLength/@example rather than plain string.

patterns.name: Explicit visibility on every mutable field
patterns.description: Party model fields each declare @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update) or a subset — no implicit all-lifecycle fields. id is Read only; key is Read+Create.

patterns.name: friendlyName for SDK type naming
patterns.description: Models use @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") to control generated SDK names and avoid collisions with Shared namespace types.

patterns.name: Intentional GOBL omissions documented in comments
patterns.description: party.tsp carries a block comment listing GOBL party fields deliberately excluded (identities, people, inboxes, emails, websites, telephones, registration, logos, ext) with rationale. Do not silently add them.
patterns.example: // Omitted: identities (maintained by apps), people, inboxes, emails, websites, telephones, registration, logos, ext

key_file_guides.role: BillingParty, BillingPartyAddresses, BillingPartyTaxIdentity — the supplier/customer party models used on invoices.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Many GOBL fields are intentionally omitted per the leading comment block — do not add them without design discussion. BillingPartyAddresses currently has only billing_address.

key_file_guides.role: Defines only the InvoiceNumber scalar (namespace Invoices). Full invoice body models live in the billing/ namespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add full invoice models here — they belong in billing/. This is a thin type definition only.
No namespace declaration in this index.tsp — callers reference types by their fully qualified Invoices.* name.

anti_patterns: Adding full invoice CRUD operations here — operations belong in the billing/ folder's operations.tsp.
Adding GOBL fields documented as intentionally omitted (identities, logos, registration, ext, etc.) without design discussion.
Defining party types without @friendlyName — generates SDK names that clash with Shared namespace types.
Using plain string instead of the InvoiceNumber scalar for invoice-number fields.

decisions.decision: BillingParty is kept intentionally minimal versus the full GOBL party spec.
decisions.rationale: Many GOBL fields (identity codes, people, inboxes, websites, logos) are not needed for OpenMeter billing and are explicitly excluded to avoid API surface bloat.

code_examples.scenario: Adding an optional address to BillingPartyAddresses
